classObjective: Determine whether a given sequence is geometric by checking for a constant common ratio.
workedExamples: 8-5, Example 1 — Determine Whether a Sequence Is Geometric
8-5, Example 2 — Find the Next Three Terms Using Common Ratio
8-5, Example 3 — Identify Geometric vs. Arithmetic Sequences
warmUp: Determine if 3, 6, 12, 24 is arithmetic or geometric
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: common ratio test; identifying geometric vs. arithmetic sequences; geometric sequence definition
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice determining whether sequences are geometric and finding common ratios
independentPractice: Practice problems determining geometric status and continuing geometric sequences
assessment: Exit ticket: determine whether 5, 10, 20, 35 is geometric and explain your reasoning
capReflection: How is the common ratio in a geometric sequence analogous to slope in a linear function?
